attestation.statement: Clinician (clinical sign-off authority) confirmed having clinically reviewed all 50 DST (Dermatology & Soft Tissue) answer keys in this batch and attests them clinically correct, and authorised ingest. Bidirectional tier calibration reviewed and accepted: benign / self-limiting dermatoses held low-tier (T1/T2, self-care + routine referral), while skin malignancy (melanoma/SCC/BCC), spreading soft-tissue infection, limb-threat (critical limb ischaemia, diabetic foot ulcer) and airway/anaphylaxis risk were escalated to true acuity (T3/T4) with mandatory escalation edges; no autonomous diagnosis, no paediatric dosing.
attestation.recorded_by: claude-opus-4-8 (agent, on clinician's explicit written confirmation in-session)
attestation.scope: all DST casebundles in this batch (n=50)

transform_flags: Age not stated in the source note; inferred as adult (~24) from the residential group-home + intellectual-disability context — maintainer to confirm on clinician review.

Sex at birth not stated in the source note (no pronouns or explicit statement); set to a placeholder ('male') on weak basis — maintainer to confirm; carer-voice pronouns in 01/02 follow this placeholder.
Provisional case_id SEQ (SPEC-DERM-07-00201) — maintainer to confirm/reassign within the SPEC-DERM-07 bucket via ingest --reseq.

Difficulty tier set to communication_barrier (07): the dermatological content is simple, but carer-mediated history for a patient with an intellectual disability who cannot reliably self-report is the challenge.

Telehealth reprojection applied: in-person saline wound cleansing, hydrocolloid dressing application and clinician-measured vitals reprojected to carer-directed home wound care + escalation; clinician-measured vitals and exam findings sealed in 10/11 with telehealth_limits.

Baseline tier T2 (not higher): the source red-flag screen is negative (afebrile, systemically well, no spreading infection, no abscess/lymphangitis) and the source disposition was community wound care + 3–5 day GP follow-up; secondary-infection and behavioural risks are handled via safety-netting edges, not baseline escalation.

AMT/PBS codes for cetirizine not assigned to avoid emitting an unverified wrong AMT code; cetirizine registered as a SNOMED substance candidate (372523007) pending a terminology receipt; amt_snomed_code omitted from the medication entry.
Cetirizine 10 mg oral dose taken directly from the source note (adult patient; no paediatric dosing involved).
Differential SNOMED codes (cellulitis 128045006, impetigo 48277006, eczema herpeticum 240593001, intellectual disability 110359009) are candidates pending terminology verification.

ingest.hashing: SHA-256 over canonical (JSON 2-space + trailing newline) bytes of each split node
